data.title: As a DSO orchestrator, the worktree-isolation-guard allows authorized worktree dispatch
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update worktree-isolation-guard from categorical deny to auth-file allowlist. Guard exists in TWO locations (standalone script and session-misc-functions.sh function) — both must be updated.
**Why**: The guard currently blocks all isolation:worktree dispatch; this is the hard blocker for the entire epic
**Scope**:
- IN: worktree-isolation-guard.sh, session-misc-functions.sh hook_worktree_isolation_guard function, auth file lifecycle (/tmp/worktree-isolation-authorized-<session-uuid>), test-worktree-isolation-guard.sh updates, marker cleanup on session end
- OUT: Dispatch logic, config key, review/commit workflow

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, orchestrator can write an auth marker with session-specific entropy (UUID, not repo-root-derived) and sub-agents dispatched with isolation:worktree pass the guard when the marker exists
  ← Satisfies: "Worktree-isolation-guard updated to auth-file allowlist"
- When this story is complete, sub-agents dispatched with isolation:worktree WITHOUT an auth marker are blocked by the guard (categorical deny preserved for unauthorized use)
  ← Satisfies: "Worktree-isolation-guard updated... review/commit sub-agents that need shared state continue to be blocked"
- When this story is complete, BOTH guard implementations (standalone script AND session-misc-functions.sh) are updated and kept in sync
  ← Satisfies: "Worktree-isolation-guard updated"
- When this story is complete, auth marker is cleaned up on session end (including crash recovery — stale markers with no owning PID are removed)
  ← Satisfies: "Worktree-isolation-guard updated"
- When this story is complete, existing 40 sub-agent guard tests in test-sub-agent-guard.sh are updated to reflect the new allowlist behavior and all pass
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Security] Auth marker must include session-specific UUID — not repo root hash — to prevent concurrent session collision in /tmp
- [Security] Guard validates marker was created by current session (PID check or UUID match)
- [Maintainability] This is a safeguard file edit (CLAUDE.md rule 18) — requires explicit user authorization
- [Testing] Existing 40 sub-agent guard tests assert categorical denial — must be updated for allowlist behavior

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
